response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires deep, current C++/STL expertise for industrial automation, yet his only professional C++ work was a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His career centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with no exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle practices, MQTT, or industrial protocols. His low-latency event-driven systems work is adjacent but not a substitute for the core requirements.
strengths: Broad, seasoned engineering background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency async I/O services handling millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship, testing culture, and demonstrated mentoring/teaching ability
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience (~7 months a decade ago); C++ absent from stated skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience whatsoever
Career trajectory is firmly web/product-focused, suggesting poor fit and retention risk for an industrial C++ role
